jokes.text: D'oh! [Homer screams after car crash]
jokes.setup: Opening couch gag sequence ending with car crash
jokes.punchline: Homer's signature frustrated exclamation
jokes.explanation: Iconic Homer catchphrase that defines the character. Perfect economy and integration but not particularly original anymore.

jokes.text: I did until we came here last year.
jokes.setup: Teacher asks who's wondered how the post office works
jokes.punchline: Bart's deadpan response killing enthusiasm
jokes.explanation: Good economical setup/punchline about boring field trips, nicely delivered with Bart's attitude.

jokes.text: Wow! It's ours?
jokes.setup: Postmaster says 'Welcome to your post office'
jokes.punchline: Bart takes the possessive literally
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective misdirection joke playing on language ambiguity.

jokes.text: 'Legends of comedy,' my tuchus! What has Fatty Arbuckle done that I haven't done?
jokes.setup: Comic Book Guy presumably reading something about comedy legends
jokes.punchline: Comparing himself favorably to silent film star
jokes.explanation: Perfect Comic Book Guy arrogance with obscure cultural reference. Good character writing.

jokes.text: postalistic
jokes.setup: Guide explaining post office services
jokes.punchline: Made-up word 'postalistic' instead of 'postal'
jokes.explanation: Mild wordplay malapropism, fits dopey government worker character

jokes.text: Those are citizen relocation codes. With any luck, we'll never need 'em.
jokes.setup: Lisa asks about the extra 4 digits in zip codes
jokes.punchline: Sinister implication about government tracking/relocation
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about government surveillance disguised as innocent postal information. Clever and unsettling.

jokes.text: She's onto us. Should I flood the chamber? - Not yet. Let's get some lunch.
jokes.setup: Previous joke about citizen relocation codes
jokes.punchline: Casual discussion of sinister countermeasures
jokes.explanation: Great escalation of the previous dark humor with casual tone making it funnier.

jokes.text: You ever gone on a killing spree?
jokes.setup: Teacher asks for questions about postal work
jokes.punchline: Bart's inappropriate question about postal worker stereotype
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart question - inappropriate but logical given postal worker stereotypes. Great shock value and character consistency.

jokes.text: The day of the gun-toting, disgruntled postman shooting up the place went out with the Macarena.
jokes.setup: Responding to Bart's killing spree question
jokes.punchline: Comparing violent postal workers to a dated dance craze
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurd comparison making light of serious topic. The Macarena reference dates it but makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Well, I'm just glad I work at an elementary school.
jokes.setup: Discussion of workplace violence ending
jokes.punchline: Skinner's oblivious comment about school safety
jokes.explanation: Dark irony about school violence with perfect Skinner obliviousness. Well-structured follow-up.

jokes.text: Bingo! Birthday card! - Graduation! - Ding-ding-ding! Wedding!
jokes.setup: Dead letter office where workers gather to unwind
jokes.punchline: Workers playing with undelivered mail like a game show
jokes.explanation: Good visual gag about postal worker entertainment being other people's lost mail. Moderately clever.

jokes.text: I got some dog food! I got my letter to Santa.
jokes.setup: Kids taking undeliverable mail as souvenirs
jokes.punchline: Bart gets dog food, Lisa gets her own letter to Santa
jokes.explanation: Nice character-appropriate selections. Lisa getting her own letter back is mildly ironic.

jokes.text: A coupon book? What am I gonna do with this piece of junk?
jokes.setup: Bart dismisses coupon book as junk
jokes.punchline: Sets up Homer's obsession with it
jokes.explanation: Good setup for Homer's reaction, showing generational difference in coupon appreciation.

jokes.text: Doughy's has terrible pizza. - Yeah, but there's two!
jokes.setup: Marge points out the pizza place is terrible
jokes.punchline: Homer's logic that quantity trumps quality
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer logic - perfectly economical and character-consistent. Quality vs. quantity humor.

jokes.text: Oh, Marge. That's just a trick to get you in there, so they can cure your foot pain.
jokes.setup: Free foot pain analysis offer
jokes.punchline: Homer thinks the 'trick' is providing actual medical help
jokes.explanation: Brilliant inversion of expectations - Homer sees helping someone as the deceptive part. Great character logic.

jokes.text: Me and my Val-U-Qual book are gonna paint the town red... with savings!
jokes.setup: Homer's excitement about using coupons
jokes.punchline: Subverts 'paint the town red' with 'savings'
jokes.explanation: Nice subversion of common phrase with Homer's coupon enthusiasm. Well-delivered.

jokes.text: And I'll cap it off with a smooth, refreshing colonic.
jokes.setup: Homer planning his coupon adventure
jokes.punchline: Describes medical procedure like a beverage
jokes.explanation: Absurd description making medical procedure sound appetizing. Great shock value and Homer's oblivious enthusiasm.

jokes.text: You hear that clunk? - No. - That tells me you need four new tires.
jokes.setup: Worker claims to hear a concerning sound
jokes.punchline: Homer admits he doesn't hear it but worker continues diagnosis
jokes.explanation: Classic mechanic scam humor. Well-structured with Homer's honesty making it funnier.

jokes.text: You gonna let this man drive out of here on unsafe tires? - No, boss, I swear. - That's it. You're fired!
jokes.setup: Boss appears to catch worker in scam
jokes.punchline: Boss fires worker for not scamming hard enough
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection - boss seems ethical but is actually ensuring the scam works. Good escalation.

jokes.text: Oh, if I could only turn back the clock and buy four new tires.
jokes.setup: Homer feels guilty about worker being 'fired'
jokes.punchline: Homer's solution is exactly what the scammers wanted
jokes.explanation: Homer's gullibility and guilt making him the perfect mark. Good character consistency.

jokes.text: You came in for the free wheel balance and now it's costing you 500 simoleons. - Six with the tip.
jokes.setup: Wally recognizes the scam situation
jokes.punchline: Homer adds tip to his overcharge
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the scam with Homer's polite tipping making it worse. Also introduces 'simoleons' currency joke.

jokes.text: alignment, shocks, Armor All, stem lube. - [Laughing] Stem lube.
jokes.setup: Wally lists services he was sold
jokes.punchline: Homer laughs at 'stem lube' like it's dirty
jokes.explanation: Juvenile humor but fits Homer's character. Simple but effective immature reaction.

jokes.text: Even I didn't fall for that, although winter is coming.
jokes.setup: Homer claims he didn't fall for stem lube
jokes.punchline: Immediately shows he's considering it
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer contradiction - claims superiority then shows he's equally gullible. Great structure.

jokes.text: Oh, don't remind me. Friends helping friends, my ass!
jokes.setup: Recognition from pyramid scheme
jokes.punchline: Wally's bitter memory of the scam slogan
jokes.explanation: Good callback to MLM/pyramid scheme culture. Wally's cynicism is well-placed.

jokes.text: Bronco Nagurski didn't get no bye weeks. And now he's dead. Well, maybe they're a good thing.
jokes.setup: TV discussing bye weeks in football
jokes.punchline: Barney's flawed logic connecting no bye weeks to death
jokes.explanation: Perfect drunk logic from Barney. The immediate reversal of position based on flawed reasoning is brilliant.

jokes.text: Me? Nah. [Gasps] Unless there's a coupon for it. Nah.
jokes.setup: Asked about going to Super Bowl
jokes.punchline: Homer briefly considers it only if there's a coupon
jokes.explanation: Nice callback to Homer's coupon obsession. Shows his priorities and thought process perfectly.

jokes.text: Dang! That was my last quarterback. Now what am I gonna do? You! - Me? - Yeah, you. Get your hand off my wife's leg.
jokes.setup: Wally seems to be recruiting Homer for something important
jokes.punchline: Actually just wants him to stop inappropriate touching
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection - builds up Homer as solution then reveals uncomfortable truth about his behavior.

jokes.text: Oh, absolutely. My favorite team's in it. The Atlanta Falcons. Yeah, ever since I was a boy, I've always loved the Atlanta Falcons.
jokes.setup: Moe claiming longtime fandom
jokes.punchline: Over-emphasizing his 'lifelong' love for team that moved recently
jokes.explanation: Good sports fan bandwagon humor. Moe's overcompensation makes the lie obvious.

jokes.text: Yeah, I hear that President Clinton is gonna be watching with his wife, Hillary.
jokes.setup: Casual conversation about the Super Bowl
jokes.punchline: Barney stating obvious information as if it's insider knowledge
jokes.explanation: Mild humor about stating obvious facts as if they're special knowledge. Fits Barney's character.

jokes.text: Come on, Lenny. I need four more guys to fill my Super Bowl bus. - What do you say? - Nah. - Come on! - Nah. - Oh, come on! - Ah- Yes!
jokes.setup: Homer trying to convince Lenny to join
jokes.punchline: Persistence paying off with minimal effort
jokes.explanation: Simple escalation humor showing how people can be worn down. Functional but not exceptional.

jokes.text: Yes! Now that Lenny's in, Carl will fall like a domino.
jokes.setup: Homer's recruiting strategy
jokes.punchline: Understanding of peer pressure dynamics
jokes.explanation: Homer showing surprising understanding of social dynamics. Good character moment.

jokes.text: My dream has always been to see the Bolshoi Ballet.
jokes.setup: Homer excited about football trip
jokes.punchline: Marge's completely different cultural interests
jokes.explanation: Good character contrast between Marge's sophistication and Homer's interests. Simple but effective.

jokes.text: Yeah, yeah. Do we have any pencils that work?
jokes.setup: Marge sharing her cultural dreams
jokes.punchline: Homer's complete disinterest and mundane concern
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer dismissal of Marge's interests. Great economy and character consistency.

jokes.text: Yeah, not a dame in sight. Oh, thank God. Now we can stop holding it in. [All Sighing]
jokes.setup: Comic Book Guy celebrates no women on trip
jokes.punchline: Men collectively releasing gas they'd been holding
jokes.explanation: Juvenile but effective group humor about male behavior. Well-executed gross-out comedy.

jokes.text: Aw, crap. It's that pip-squeak Rudy.
jokes.setup: Someone approaching the bus
jokes.punchline: Homer's dismissive reaction to small person
jokes.explanation: Sets up underdog character dynamic. Homer's casual cruelty is in character.

jokes.text: Forget it, kid. You're too small to go to the Super Bowl. But what I lack in size I make up for in... obnoxiousness. [All Cheering]
jokes.setup: Rudy seems to be making case for inclusion
jokes.punchline: His selling point is being annoying
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection - expects positive trait but delivers negative one that the group actually wants.

jokes.text: Or me. Better ask me.
jokes.setup: Introducing Homer as team leader while he's drunk
jokes.punchline: Wally's immediate course correction
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy in recognizing Homer's current state. Simple but effective.

jokes.text: Yeah, I'm bored too.
jokes.setup: Marge commenting on peaceful weekend
jokes.punchline: Lisa's honest response to 'peaceful'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lisa honesty puncturing Marge's attempt at positivity. Great economy.

jokes.text: It's so rigid and uncreative. - Okay.
jokes.setup: Lisa critiquing paint by numbers
jokes.punchline: Marge's immediate acceptance of criticism
jokes.explanation: Good character dynamic with Marge's accommodating nature and Lisa's intellectual standards.

jokes.text: Oh, leather craft. - Oh, those poor, helpless cows!
jokes.setup: Looking through craft options
jokes.punchline: Lisa's empathy for animals
jokes.explanation: Consistent with Lisa's character values. Simple but character-appropriate.

jokes.text: What about clay? You got any problem with clay?
jokes.setup: Lisa rejecting multiple craft options
jokes.punchline: Marge's frustrated search for acceptable option
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Marge's frustration with Lisa's standards. Well-delivered.

jokes.text: Vincent Price's Egg Magic. Wow. What are we waiting for?
jokes.setup: Lisa being picky about crafts
jokes.punchline: Immediately excited by Vincent Price association
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to Lisa's pickiness - celebrity endorsement wins her over. Sets up larger storyline.

jokes.text: All right, all right. You guys have had way too much booze. Last call.
jokes.setup: Group getting rowdy on bus
jokes.punchline: Tour guide calling 'last call' like a bartender
jokes.explanation: Good situational humor applying bar rules to bus travel. Functional comedy.

jokes.text: The guys made kind of a mess in your bathroom. - What bathroom?
jokes.setup: Homer apologizing for bathroom mess
jokes.punchline: Revelation there was no bathroom to mess up
jokes.explanation: Great setup/punchline with horrifying implications. Excellent economy and shock value.

jokes.text: I don't wanna be a Panicky Pete, but it's Sunday and I haven't been to church yet.
jokes.setup: Ned expressing concern
jokes.punchline: His worry is about missing church at the Super Bowl
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ned character moment - finding religious concern even at sporting event. 'Panicky Pete' is good characterization.

jokes.text: No problem. The N.F.L.'s got you covered.
jokes.setup: Ned's church concern
jokes.punchline: NFL providing religious services
jokes.explanation: Good observation about corporate accommodation. Sets up religious service at game.

jokes.text: And that the wandering Oakland Raiders may someday find a home.
jokes.setup: NFL church service prayer
jokes.punchline: Prayer for nomadic football team
jokes.explanation: Clever sports humor about team relocations framed as religious concern. Good cultural reference.

jokes.text: Cool! The N.F.L.'s oldest surviving player. - I'm 53 years young.
jokes.setup: Bart excited about 'oldest surviving player'
jokes.punchline: Player is only 53
jokes.explanation: Good joke about NFL career longevity. The 'years young' language adds to the humor.

jokes.text: So, Ned, you like dune buggies? Well, not my cup of- Sure you do. Everyone likes dune buggies.
jokes.setup: Homer asking Ned about dune buggies
jokes.punchline: Overriding Ned's polite refusal with assertion
jokes.explanation: Good Homer bulldozing of Ned's preferences. Shows Homer's tendency to impose his interests.

jokes.text: Excuse me. Coming through. Friends of Dan Marino.
jokes.setup: Homer trying to get through crowd
jokes.punchline: Claiming false celebrity connection
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer scheme to get special treatment. Simple but effective character moment.

jokes.text: I'm catching a pass from Dan Marino. This is the greatest moment of my- Whoo-hoo! Homer Simpson with a pick!
jokes.setup: Bart excited about catching pass from 'Dan Marino'
jokes.punchline: Homer intercepting pass meant for Bart
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy with Homer stealing his son's moment. Character-consistent selfishness.

jokes.text: Oh, honey, that is egg-ceptional.
jokes.setup: Looking at Lisa's egg craft project
jokes.punchline: Marge's enthusiastic egg pun
jokes.explanation: Simple pun fitting Marge's supportive mom character. Not particularly clever but character-appropriate.

jokes.text: I can't believe Vincent Price would lend his name to such a shoddy product.
jokes.setup: Missing parts from Vincent Price craft kit
jokes.punchline: Lisa's faith in celebrity endorsement quality
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Lisa's trust in authority figures. Sets up phone call surprise.

jokes.text: Mom, this was made in 1967. They're probably out of business.
jokes.setup: Marge wanting to call company about missing parts
jokes.punchline: Lisa's logical assessment of 30-year-old product
jokes.explanation: Good Lisa logic contrasting with Marge's optimism. Sets up surprise that company still exists.

jokes.text: Hello. This is Vincent Price. [Gasps] It's Vincent Price! I thought he was dead.
jokes.setup: Calling old company number expecting disconnection
jokes.punchline: Vincent Price answers, followed by Lisa's morbid honesty
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection with celebrity still being alive, plus Lisa's innocent but morbid comment. Well-structured surprise.

jokes.text: You should know the grave could never tame me. [Sinister Laughter]
jokes.setup: Lisa's comment about thinking he was dead
jokes.punchline: Price's dramatic, horror-movie response
jokes.explanation: Perfect Vincent Price parody staying in horror character even for mundane customer service. Great voice work reference.

jokes.text: And now I must return to the sweet embrace of the crypt. But I'll be back. [Sinister Laughter]
jokes.setup: Ending customer service call
jokes.punchline: Price maintaining horror persona for mundane goodbye
jokes.explanation: Great commitment to character voice work. Perfect blend of horror tropes with customer service.

jokes.text: So, is he alive or not?
jokes.setup: Vincent Price's cryptic messages about death and crypts
jokes.punchline: Marge's practical confusion about his actual status
jokes.explanation: Perfect Marge practicality cutting through the horror theatrics. Great economy and character contrast.

jokes.text: Scalping tickets to the Super Bowl. Have you no shame, sir? I should give you a royal caning.
jokes.setup: Encountering ticket scalper
jokes.punchline: Sea Captain's nautical outrage and threat
jokes.explanation: Good Sea Captain character moment with nautical language. 'Royal caning' is nice period-appropriate threat.

jokes.text: Yeah, see, the hologram's missing and there's no such team as the Spungos and finally, these seem to be printed on some sort of cracker.
jokes.setup: Checking obviously fake tickets
jokes.punchline: Escalating ridiculousness of counterfeiting attempts
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation from technical to absurd problems. 'Spungos' is a great fake team name.

jokes.text: Stop eating our tickets!
jokes.setup: Security guard noting tickets are printed on crackers
jokes.punchline: Guard eating the evidence
jokes.explanation: Great escalation of the cracker joke with guard actually eating them. Perfect physical comedy setup.

jokes.text: Yes, if by 'crestfallen' you mean 'kill us'.
jokes.setup: Homer saying the guys will be crestfallen about fake tickets
jokes.punchline: Wally correcting with violent reality
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from mild disappointment to mortal danger. Well-structured euphemism correction.

jokes.text: Have I told you that I love you? Forget it. You'll just hurt me like all the others.
jokes.setup: Homer desperately trying to get scalper's help
jokes.punchline: Scalper's romantic rejection and emotional baggage
jokes.explanation: Unexpected emotional depth from minor character. Good subversion of typical scalper interaction.

jokes.text: Ow! - [All] Yea! As a doctor, I'd say he's had enough. But as a football fan- [Cheer Building Up] [Screams]
jokes.setup: Player being injured on field
jokes.punchline: Dr. Hibbert's conflicted professional vs. fan ethics
jokes.explanation: Great character conflict between professional duty and sports fandom. Dark but perfectly executed.

jokes.text: The Catholic Church- we've made a few changes:
jokes.setup: Super Bowl commercial
jokes.punchline: Catholic Church treating doctrine changes like product updates
jokes.explanation: Good satire of corporate advertising applied to religious institutions. Clever cultural commentary.

jokes.text: These Super Bowl commercials are weird.
jokes.setup: Catholic Church advertisement
jokes.punchline: Homer's understated reaction to surreal commercial
jokes.explanation: Perfect understatement for absurd situation. Homer's casual reaction makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Real. Real. Real. Real. Real. So by the process of elimination, this one is the fake. [Groaning] That's painful.
jokes.setup: Testing jail bars for the fake one
jokes.punchline: His 'logical' method fails spectacularly
jokes.explanation: Great payoff to Comic Book Guy's false expertise. Perfect physical comedy with logical fallacy.

jokes.text: No, no, no. I know my roars. And that was most definitely a safety.
jokes.setup: Crowd cheering sounds like touchdown
jokes.punchline: Homer's confident expertise in crowd noise interpretation
jokes.explanation: Great Homer expertise in absurd area. 'I know my roars' is perfectly confident and ridiculous.

jokes.text: Gosh, fellas, to see you all stuck in here when even guys in China can watch all the action from their town squares or what have you, well, I just feel pretty doggone bad.
jokes.setup: Group missing the game in jail
jokes.punchline: Ned's inadvertent rubbing in of their situation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ned attempt at sympathy that makes situation worse. Good character obliviousness.

jokes.text: If they were electing a president of Dumbville, I'd have to nominate me.
jokes.setup: Ned feeling guilty about their situation
jokes.punchline: Ned's folksy self-deprecation
jokes.explanation: Good Ned self-deprecation with his characteristic wholesome language. 'Dumbville' is perfectly Ned.

jokes.text: Me? I'm Dolly Parton. - I didn't ask for your life story. Just give me the key.
jokes.setup: Homer asking cleaning lady for help
jokes.punchline: It's actually Dolly Parton, but Homer doesn't care
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection with celebrity cameo and Homer's perfect dismissive response. Excellent character moment.

jokes.text: Yeah. I book a lot of package tours to Dollywood and EuroDollywood. - That's in Alabama.
jokes.setup: Wally knowing Dolly Parton
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of European Dollywood theme park
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurd expansion of Dollywood concept. 'That's in Alabama' adds perfect deadpan geography.

jokes.text: You guys are following me? I was following Flanders.
jokes.setup: Group lost and asking Homer for direction
jokes.punchline: Nobody actually knows where they're going
jokes.explanation: Good revelation of group incompetence. Classic leadership failure with Homer logic.

jokes.text: I'm Rupert Murdoch, the billionaire tyrant. And this is my skybox.
jokes.setup: Confronting trespassers in skybox
jokes.punchline: Murdoch's self-aware description as 'tyrant'
jokes.explanation: Good self-aware media mogul satire. 'Billionaire tyrant' is perfectly blunt self-description.

jokes.text: Tell you what, Mr. Murdoch. Let's just split the difference. The boys and I will just crouch here quietly and take it easy on the snacks- Silence!
jokes.setup: Homer trying to negotiate staying in skybox
jokes.punchline: Murdoch's authoritarian shutdown
jokes.explanation: Good Homer attempt at reasonable compromise vs. media power. Shows different approaches to conflict.

jokes.text: Whoo! I'm going to Disneyland! Really? 'Cause I'm a travel agent and I've heard nothing but bad things.
jokes.setup: Traditional Super Bowl victory declaration
jokes.punchline: Wally's practical travel agent perspective
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of classic sports trope with Wally's professional expertise contradicting celebration.

jokes.text: Hello. This is President Clinton. - Hey, how you doing? Your determination and grit under extreme pressure are an inspiration.
jokes.setup: President calling to congratulate
jokes.punchline: Homer's casual response to presidential praise
jokes.explanation: Good Homer casualness with authority figures. His informal response to presidential praise is perfect character.

jokes.text: Hello? Hello? I command you to answer me! Hello?
jokes.setup: Homer hanging up during presidential call
jokes.punchline: President's frustrated authoritarian response to dial tone
jokes.explanation: Good payoff showing presidential authority has limits. Clinton's commands to dial tone are perfectly frustrated.

jokes.text: I'll probably give it to my wife. It's our anniversary today. [All Sighing]
jokes.setup: Discussing what to do with Super Bowl ring
jokes.punchline: Carl's sweet gesture making everyone feel guilty
jokes.explanation: Good emotional contrast with group's selfishness. Carl's sweetness highlights others' moral failing.

jokes.text: 'Cause you can really 'go places' in the travel business. - Huh? Feel free to use that one. - What one?
jokes.setup: Wally suggesting Homer become travel agent
jokes.punchline: Wally's proud pun completely missed by Homer
jokes.explanation: Good character dynamics with Wally's dad joke pride and Homer's obliviousness. Well-executed missed connection.

jokes.text: Did it strike you as odd that in a Super Bowl show with Dolly Parton we didn't see any football or singing?
jokes.setup: Continuing sports-style episode analysis
jokes.punchline: Pointing out the episode's failure to deliver on its premise
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-criticism of the episode's own structure. Self-aware acknowledgment of narrative shortcomings.

jokes.text: What a way to treat the loyal fans who've put up with so much nonsense from this franchise.
jokes.setup: Criticizing the episode as rip-off
jokes.punchline: Treating The Simpsons like disappointing sports franchise
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-humor comparing TV show to sports team with disappointing fans. Self-aware and bold.

jokes.text: Now, I'll tell you- that doesn't make a lick of sense! I know. Just get on the bus.
jokes.setup: Vincent Price appearing as bus driver
jokes.punchline: Homer acknowledging plot makes no sense but accepting it
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-humor with Homer as audience surrogate acknowledging absurd plotting while accepting it. Perfect economy and self-awareness.
